summary: Gruber announces giveaways to promote the Daring Fireball membership drive. Brent Simmons has donated a full NetNewsWire license, and Gruber has Gmail invitations to distribute. The NetNewsWire license will go to a random paying member after the drive ends on July 1, while Gmail invitations are available to anyone who has donated any amount. The post serves as a straightforward promotional push to encourage donations during the membership drive.
key_points: Brent Simmons donated a NetNewsWire 1.x license that will also cover the upcoming free 2.0 update
Gmail invitations were still a scarce, desirable commodity worth using as giveaway incentives
The giveaway structure creates two tiers: full members eligible for the software license, any-amount donors eligible for Gmail invites
Community support from other Mac bloggers helped boost the Gmail invitation pool from 2 to 25
money_quotes: Brent Simmons has generously offered a full license to the award-winning NetNewsWire 1.x for my use in promoting the Daring Fireball membership drive.
I've got a couple of Gmail invitations burning a hole in my pocket.
The NetNewsWire license will be awarded July 1 to one random member after the membership drive is over.
If you've already joined, you're already entered.
I now have a staggering 25 Gmail invitations to give away.
key_insight: In 2004, a Gmail invitation and a $25 RSS reader license were valuable enough incentives to drive a blog membership campaign, reflecting the scarcity economics of the early web 2.0 era.
